2604.14258.title: GFT: From Imitation to Reward Fine-Tuning with Unbiased Group Advantages and Dynamic Coefficient Rectification
2604.14258.paper_summary: Large language models are typically post-trained using supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL), yet effectively unifying efficient knowledge injection with robust generalization remains challenging. In this work, we provide a training-dynamics analysis showing that SFT can be interpreted as a special case of policy gradient optimization with an extremely sparse implicit reward and unstable inverse-probability weighting, which together lead to single-path dependency, entropy collapse, and gradient explosion. Motivated by this diagnosis, we propose Group Fine-Tuning (GFT), a unified post-training framework that addresses these intrinsic limitations through two mechanisms: Group Advantage Learning, which constructs diverse response groups and derives normalized contrastive supervision to alleviate reward sparsity, and Dynamic Coefficient Rectification, which adaptively bounds inverse-probability weights to stabilize optimization while preserving efficient knowledge injection. Experiments demonstrate that GFT consistently surpasses SFT-based methods and yields policies that integrate more smoothly with subsequent RL training.
2604.14258.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型通常通過監督微調（SFT）和強化學習（RL）進行後訓練，但有效地將高效知識注入與穩健的泛化統一仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提供了一個訓練動態分析，顯示SFT可以被解釋為一種特殊情況的策略梯度優化，具有極為稀疏的隱式獎勵和不穩定的逆概率加權，這共同導致了單一路徑依賴、熵崩潰和梯度爆炸。<lb/>受到這一診斷的啟發，我們提出了群體微調（GFT），這是一個統一的後訓練框架，通過兩個機制解決這些內在限制：群體優勢學習，該機制構建多樣的反應群體並推導出標準化的對比監督，以減輕獎勵稀疏性；動態係數修正，該機制自適應地限制逆概率權重，以穩定優化，同時保持高效的知識注入。<lb/>實驗表明，GFT始終超越基於SFT的方法，並產生與隨後的RL訓練更平滑集成的策略。
2604.14258.author: Wangjie Gan et.al.
2604.14258.authors: Wangjie Gan, Miao Pan, Linbo Xi, Wenqi Zhang, Jintao Chen, Jianwei Yin, Xuhong Zhang
